source.title: PDD Holdings Owner Earnings and FCF Trend Analysis

extractions.claim: PDD's FCF has compounded at 42% annually over 5 years, reaching $16.6B in FY2024, before declining slightly to $15.6B TTM reflecting heavy investment cycle
extractions.evidence: Annual FCF: FY2023 $13.18B (+89.9% YoY), FY2024 $16.572B (+25.7% YoY). TTM FCF (Sept 2025): $15.623B. 5-year average FCF/share growth: 42.3%/year. 3-year average FCF/share growth: 58.9%/year. FCF per share TTM: $10.52.

verification.verifier_notes: FCF data from MacroTrends and GuruFocus. Growth rates confirmed.

extractions.claim: Owner earnings approximation: TTM FCF $15.6B minus SBC $1.4B = ~$14.2B true owner earnings. PDD's capex is minimal (asset-light model), so FCF closely approximates owner earnings.
extractions.evidence: TTM FCF: $15.623B. Annual SBC (2024): $1.354B. Adjusted owner earnings: ~$14.3B. PDD is an asset-light platform — minimal PP&E requirements. Operating cash flow Q3 2025 alone was RMB 45.7B ($6.4B), significantly higher than RMB 27.5B in Q3 2024 (+66% YoY).

verification.verifier_notes: Owner earnings is FCF minus SBC as a rough approximation. A more precise calculation would also adjust for working capital timing (merchant deposits create float income) and maintenance vs growth capex. Given PDD's minimal capex, FCF minus SBC is a reasonable proxy.

extractions.claim: PDD's operating cash flow surged 66% YoY in Q3 2025 to RMB 45.7B despite revenue growing only 9% — demonstrating powerful operating leverage even during investment cycle
extractions.evidence: Q3 2025 operating cash flow: RMB 45,660.5M ($6.4B), vs RMB 27,522.3M in Q3 2024 — a 66% increase. Revenue grew 9% YoY to RMB 108.3B. Net income grew 17% YoY to RMB 29.3B. This divergence between revenue growth (9%) and OCF growth (66%) suggests improving cash conversion efficiency.

verification.verifier_notes: Figures from PDD Q3 2025 earnings release. The OCF surge may partly reflect working capital timing rather than pure operating improvement.
